Monday.subject: History & Geog 8 (Old World & America) Syllabus
Monday.assignment_text: Today re-write the story you told yesterday, but do it from the point of view of someone else in the story. If you had written about David and Goliath yesterday from David’s point of view, then today you would tell it from Goliath’s. If you told your story from the third person, that is, as an observer rather than one of the characters, today tell it from the point of view of one of the characters.

Monday.subject: Poetry 8 Syllabus
Monday.assignment_text:
Start memorizing “The Merchant of Venice”, Act V, Scene I, lines 53-88 (this passage begins with "How sweet the moonlight sleeps upon this bank..."). Spend five minutes each day this week working on the first 4 lines, from “How sweet the moonlight sleeps…” to “...sweet harmony.”

Review the poems you learned in Seventh Grade (see the Poetry List for Kindergarten to Eighth Grade found at the end of these lesson plans). Recite as many as you can from memory. If you have not already memorized these poems or cannot recite them, that is fine, just read and enjoy them.

Discuss the questions for Chapter 26, or write out your answers.

Spend five minutes each day this week working on the first 4 lines, from “How sweet the moonlight sleeps…” to “...sweet harmony.” Review the poems you learned in Seventh Grade (see the Poetry List for Kindergarten to Eighth Grade found at the end of these lesson plans). Recite as many as you can from memory. If you have not already memorized these poems or cannot recite them, that is fine, just read and enjoy them.
